Today: Mode 3 verifying PROP-007 and re-measuring context loads. Yesterday: Mode 2 finding 4-file FUSE cascade. Two days ago: Mode 2 discovering build.js staleness root cause. Every run has produced actionable findings.

re_work.root_cause: False positive. Mass mtime refresh on EXP-001 through EXP-007 at 2026-04-07T10:45:24 (7 files modified within 9ms) indicates a bulk filesystem copy, not 7 re-analysis runs. Likely triggered by a build.js publish sync loop. No flagged expansion has mtime newer than 2026-04-14 — the pattern froze before today.
re_work.recommended_refinement: mtime-based detection is unreliable on FUSE. Replace with 'analyst wrote a new file to analyst/expansions/ with matching id AFTER integrated_at' — requires checking git log instead of stat. Low priority since no new re-work has occurred in 3 days.

implemented_since_last_report.commit: 2026-04-16T17:02:03Z 'PROP-007: Add build.js and build-scripts utilities to OWNERSHIP table'
implemented_since_last_report.scope_note: Implementation used a narrower subset than the proposal: added digest-reviews.js, fix-json-quotes.js, archive-old-reports.js, and build.js itself. The proposal also listed sync-code-analysis.js, add-references.js, apply-patches.js, backfill-issues.js, extract-sections.js. These 5 remain unclassified. Since none have been edited recently, they are not currently stale — but the class is still open.
implemented_since_last_report.followup_needed: Low priority. Monitor whether any of the 5 unclassified build-scripts drift from main in future runs. If they do, write a PROP-007-followup to extend OWNERSHIP.

cost_engineering.cumulative_estimated_savings: PROP-007 eliminated a recurring false-positive class (~3-4 runs' worth of misplaced Opus attention per week) and hardened the workspace against silent build-script drift. PROP-005 (still pending) would cut decider no-op runs by an estimated 33-42% based on the last 3-run sample (1/3 was a true no-op minor patch that Haiku could have handled).

accuracy_findings.description: Monitoring Pipeline table (CLAUDE.md lines 103-112) lists schedules that do NOT match the actual cron expressions. Current cron: analyst '8 */8 * * *' (every 8h), curmudgeon '0 */8 * * *' (every 8h), decider '30 */8 * * *' (every 8h). CLAUDE.md says: analyst 'Every 2h', curmudgeon 'Every 4h (BAU) / 30m (churn-and-burn)', decider 'Every 4h'. The scheduled task descriptions explicitly call out 'Quiet-period 8h cadence' — the shift from BAU to quiet is intentional but the prompt table never got updated.
accuracy_findings.evidence: list_scheduled_tasks returns cron '8 */8 * * *' / '0 */8 * * *' / '30 */8 * * *' for analyst/curmudgeon/decider. CLAUDE.md lines 105-107 describe 2h/4h/4h.
accuracy_findings.recommended_fix: Either (a) change the CLAUDE.md table to show both nominal and quiet-period cadences, e.g., 'Every 8h (quiet) / every 2h (BAU)' for analyst, or (b) add a footnote explaining the pipeline auto-downshifts to 8h when consecutive_quiet_polls >= N. Option (b) is more honest about the design. Decider-side: not a tinker fix because prompt files are git-owned and decider-applied.
accuracy_findings.why_not_self_fixed: CLAUDE.md is git-owned. Edits from the FUSE workspace don't propagate to main (per the Prompt Sync Gotcha in CLAUDE.md itself) and are overwritten on the next publish. The decider or a human must apply this patch from a clean clone.

accuracy_findings.description: tinker.md Pipeline table (lines 15-17) has the same stale schedules: 'Every 12h / 2h / 4h / 4h' for poller/analyst/curmudgeon/decider. Poller IS 12h (correct); the other three are 8h under current quiet-period regime.
accuracy_findings.evidence: Direct read of monitor/prompts/tinker.md lines 15-21 shows the table entries.
accuracy_findings.recommended_fix: Same as above — update to reflect quiet-period reality or explicitly call out the two regimes. The tinker prompt explicitly says 'Verify these schedules against actual cron expressions each run. Use list_scheduled_tasks or check the task configs. If the table above is wrong, update it.' — which I am doing here by flagging it; but I cannot self-apply for the same FUSE reason.

accuracy_findings.description: decider.md lines 16-18 list schedules that match NEITHER the nominal BAU cadence NOR the actual quiet-period cadence. Says: 'Poller (every 4h)', 'Analyst (every 30min)', 'Curmudgeon (every 10min)'. This has been flagged in 6 consecutive tinker reports without correction. The phrasing ('every 30min', 'every 10min') appears to be a legacy artifact from an earlier churn-and-burn regime that was never revised when the pipeline standardized on longer cadences.
accuracy_findings.recommended_fix: Replace lines 16-18 with: '- **Poller** (every 12h): Dome site changes / - **Analyst** (every 8h quiet / every 2h BAU): Deep scientific analysis... / - **Curmudgeon** (every 8h quiet / every 4h BAU): Adversarial self-review'.
accuracy_findings.why_not_self_fixed: decider.md is in monitor/prompts/ and is git-owned. See Prompt Sync Gotcha. Recommending a PROP.

accuracy_findings.evidence: CLAUDE.md lines 58-59 reflect the PROP-007 classification. build.js OWNERSHIP matches.

self_fixes_applied.action: Updated status 'proposed' → 'implemented' per PROP Lifecycle Verification rules. Added implemented_at (2026-04-16T17:02:03Z from commit), implemented_by (auto-detected by tinker 2026-04-17T09:39Z), and actual_result describing the implementation scope.
self_fixes_applied.why_safe: PROP metadata is tinker-owned. The rule in tinker-proposals-and-fixes.md Step 2b explicitly prescribes this action when a verification_pattern returns FIXED. FUSE can handle in-place JSON edits (no unlink required).

Recommend either (a) update tables to show both regimes, or (b) add footnote describing auto-downshift to 8h on N consecutive quiet polls.

recommendations.description: PROP-005 (decider pre-flight work check) — 11th consecutive STILL_BROKEN. The pipeline is stable and quiet; the decider still produces substantive work on ~67% of runs under 8h quiet cadence. PROP-005 would cut the remaining 33% no-op runs with a Haiku gate. At this point either merge it or close it — re-escalating monthly is a tinker waste pattern in its own right.

decider.md lines 16-18 schedule descriptions have been wrong for 6 consecutive reports. Tinker cannot apply from FUSE (prompt sync gotcha). The decider — which runs from a clean clone — is the right agent to pick this up from today's self_fix_candidates_not_applied entry. Simple one-block replacement; see the proposed_find/proposed_replace there.

CLAUDE.md and tinker.md Pipeline tables have stale schedule rows. Specifically: analyst 'Every 2h' should reflect the 8h quiet-period regime, curmudgeon 'Every 4h (BAU) / 30m (churn-and-burn)' is missing the 8h quiet case, decider 'Every 4h' is actually 8h. Same FUSE constraint — decider or human applies from a clone. This is a moderate accuracy finding because these tables are what every agent reads via CLAUDE.md.

Disk at 82% is trending green for the first time in a week. git gc --auto in the new build.js appears to be working. Keep monitoring — if it regresses, the next lever is /var cleanup (apt cache, log rotation). No action needed today.

PROP-007 scope was narrower than proposed: 5 of the listed 8 build-scripts remain unclassified (sync-code-analysis.js, add-references.js, apply-patches.js, backfill-issues.js, extract-sections.js). None are currently stale, but the class is still open. If any of them drifts in a future run, write a PROP-007-followup to extend OWNERSHIP.

Analyst at 67% no-op rate under 8h quiet cadence is the next efficiency lever after PROP-005 lands. A consecutive_quiet_polls signal in status.json could gate the next analyst run similarly to how PROP-005 gates the decider. But counter-argument: analyst's Mode 4 (fingerprint hunting) is idle-work that benefits from occasional runs even with zero queue items. Not urgent — let PROP-005's human-decision cycle resolve first, then revisit.

notes_to_future_tinker: PROP-007 closed this run is a good PROP-lifecycle example: proposal → implementation → auto-verify → in-place status update. PROP-005 is the cautionary opposite — 11 escalations without resolution. Consider whether tinker should stop re-escalating after N runs and instead just cite the escalation count briefly. Also: the re-work false-positive detector (mtime > integrated_at) is noisy on FUSE because bulk copy operations refresh mtimes — consider swapping the detection for a git-log-based check in the next Mode 3 run that touches this area.
